text.title: AI-Generated Code Compliance Validation Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every application of `AI-Generated Code Compliance Validation` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Validation MUST cite a client coding-standard charter (checked-in or faithfully-summarised). No charter → load `client-conventions-reverse-engineering` first.
rule.rationale: Without a charter, lint defaults shadow client rules.

rule.statement: Lint + license + scan MUST run on the AI-touched delta only — full-repo runs explode time + noise.
rule.rationale: Full-repo runs find pre-existing issues unrelated to the AI diff.

rule.statement: Step MUST emit a license diff against client SPDX allow-list. Unknown / disallowed license → BLOCK PR.
rule.rationale: AGPL / non-allowed licenses introduced by AI-suggested deps are the most common compliance miss.

rule.statement: Per-client `forbidden-apis.yaml` declares compliance-posture restrictions (no `eval`, no unbounded `requests.get`, no console.log to STDOUT in HIPAA, etc.). Scan blocks PR on any hit.
rule.rationale: Posture rules encoded as YAML keep the scan executable and auditable.

rule.statement: Step MUST scan for secrets + PII with regex families (API keys, JWT, SSN, credit-card, email). Hits BLOCK PR.
rule.rationale: AI-suggested fixtures often inline secrets / sample PII.

rule.statement: Findings are composed into actionable PR comments with rationale + recommended fix. 'Lint failed' bare messages are rejected.
rule.rationale: Bare-lint messages get ignored; rationale + fix gets actioned.

rule.statement: Final pass requires a named senior-dev signoff. AI-shipped code never opens a client PR unreviewed.
When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
